name: polars
summary: Blazingly fast DataFrames — a modern pandas alternative written in Rust

story.problem: Pandas is slow on large files, uses too much memory, and its API accumulates footguns (SettingWithCopyWarning, implicit index, chained assignment). Processing a 1GB CSV in pandas can take minutes; polars does it in seconds.
story.mental_model: Polars uses a lazy evaluation model: you chain expressions that describe what you want, and polars figures out the optimal execution plan before touching any data. It processes data in parallel, column-by-column, using Rust under the hood. The API is stricter and more consistent than pandas — no index, no implicit behaviours.
story.when_to_use: Large datasets (hundreds of MB to GB), performance-critical ETL pipelines, or any new project where you don't have a pandas dependency. If you find yourself waiting on pandas operations, polars will usually be 10-100x faster.
story.when_not_to_use: If your codebase is deep in the pandas ecosystem (matplotlib, seaborn, sklearn all expect pandas). When the dataset is small and pandas is fast enough — switching has a learning curve.

alternatives.name: pandas
alternatives.reason: Larger ecosystem, more tutorials, integrates with everything — use for smaller data

alternatives.name: dask
alternatives.reason: Pandas API for out-of-core and distributed data — fits into existing pandas code

alternatives.name: duckdb
alternatives.reason: SQL over DataFrames and files — great for ad-hoc analysis without a database

nodes.label: polars
nodes.summary: Top-level import. Alias as `pl` by convention.

nodes.label: DataFrame
nodes.summary: Eager 2D data structure. Data is loaded and operations execute immediately.

nodes.label: LazyFrame
nodes.summary: Lazy query plan. Chain operations and call .collect() to execute — enables query optimisation.

nodes.label: Expr (expressions)
nodes.summary: The building block of polars queries. pl.col('name'), pl.lit(1), pl.sum('col'), etc.

nodes.label: read_csv()
nodes.summary: Load a CSV file. Significantly faster than pandas.read_csv for large files.

nodes.label: scan_csv()
nodes.summary: Lazy version of read_csv. Nothing is read until .collect() — compose queries first.

nodes.label: group_by()
nodes.summary: Group rows and apply aggregations. Uses parallel execution automatically.

nodes.label: join()
nodes.summary: SQL-style join of two DataFrames. Faster than pandas merge.

steps.label: Add column
steps.explanation: Expressions are composable — pl.col() returns an Expr you can chain methods on.

steps.label: Scan (lazy)
steps.explanation: Nothing is read yet — this just registers the source.

steps.label: Execute
steps.explanation: Only now does polars read the file, optimise the query plan, and execute in parallel.
